description: Legal fragment. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dated: 141[.] or 142[.] Seleucid, which is 1098–18 CE. Looks like the hand of Hillel b. Eli, in which case the date would be closer to the beginning of that range. Names: Nissim b. Avraham; Mevorakh; Abū l-Faḍl. Mentions a moneychanging shop and a sum of 6 dirhams/month. The substance of the document is difficult to determine from what remains.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-11. מוחזק בJRL.
